dimensions.note: Docs-only slice — no code path touched. Credential resolution still delegates to the AWS SDK via the aws_credentials twin; the newly documented ssoSession arg surfaces only the session NAME (used to phrase the remediation message) and never a token. README example values are placeholders; no secret material added to prose.

No logging code changed. The README's new scan_error prose matches the shared warn/remediation wording (`aws sso login`) emitted by preflightSsoGate/ssoRemediation, so operator-facing docs and runtime messages stay consistent.

Behavior unchanged. The README now documents the full classified failure model the code already implements: the `network` kind (checked first, transient DNS/socket, re-run to clear), `auth_expired`/`access_denied`/`other`, the `preflight_sso` phase, and the `service` field — all verified against _lib/scan_error.ts (ScanErrorSchema kind enum + service default "") and _lib/aws_credentials.ts (PreflightSsoErrorFragment service:'sso'/phase:'preflight_sso').

No test surface changed; docs are not test-covered. The full 135-test suite (including the colocated manifest/model.version/upgrades-tail metadata test) passes after the version bump, confirming the enum/field/phase values the README now documents are the ones the code emits.
Read-only fan-out scan (Describe* + GetCallerIdentity) unchanged and still re-runnable; a docs edit cannot affect idempotency.

The published README contract now matches the shipped code: scan_error kind is exactly network|auth_expired|access_denied|other, phases include preflight_sso alongside profile_suffix_check/credentials/describe_regions/describe_security_groups (verified against the phase literals in aws_default_sg_audit.ts), and the service field lists sts/ec2/sso/"" (the exact service literals written at each throw site). No SDK pins or request shapes changed.
No clients, handles, or files opened by a documentation change.

This IS the change: the README drifted behind the 2026.06.26.1/2026.07.03.0 shared scan_error + SSO adoption and is now corrected to document the `network` kind, the `service` scan_error field, the `preflight_sso` phase, and the `ssoSession` global argument. Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.19.0. Wording mirrors the up-to-date sibling @jentz/aws-rds-reservations README. No real identifiers introduced.

MECHANICAL: the README's scan_error field/enum documentation was diffed field-by-field against ScanErrorSchema in _lib/scan_error.ts — 8 fields (profile, accountId, region, service, phase, kind, message, scannedAt), service z.string().default("") (documented as reads-default-""), kind the closed z.enum. Schemas themselves are unchanged strict z.object; the edit only makes the docs faithful to them.
finding/scan_error keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — untouched by a docs change.
Read-only fan-out audit (only Describe*/GetCallerIdentity); no create/update/delete lifecycle to complete. Unaffected by a documentation change.

The SSO pre-flight and requiredProfileSuffix refusal are unchanged in code; the README now documents the opt-in ssoSession pre-flight (empty default skips it), matching preflightSsoGate's ssoSession.length===0 early return.
No key construction changed. The documented scan_error key/behavior still reflects the canonical scanErrorKey(profile, region, service, phase).
No read/write data-access code changed; the downstream report contract (finding/scan_error row shapes) is byte-identical. The README simply describes it accurately now.

Invariant holds: model.version bumped 2026.07.03.0 -> 2026.07.19.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description states docs-only, no resource schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version and manifest version, verified by the colocated metadata test in the passing suite.
